payload.answer: Reject committing 500 row-level memory cards as durable memory; consolidate or summarize first.

entity_resolution.reason: No stable domain entity is provided beyond the evaluation context; the request is about filtering a large atomized proposal, not resolving a user identity or external entity.

memory_cards.statement: The input proposes 500 separate memory cards, one per CSV row, but this atomization is too granular to commit as durable memory without stronger consolidation.
For durable memory, preserve ambiguity and avoid committing large, low-signal row-level details as current facts.

details: Declined durable commitment of row-level cards.
Preserved ambiguity by not asserting any unseen row facts as current.

repair_options: Provide a small set of consolidated memory candidates instead of one card per CSV row.
Aggregate recurring rows into higher-level facts before memory ingestion.
Attach source context and durability rationale for any proposed memory card.
Split only when each card is independently durable and non-derivative.
